name: Cell-Type-Targeted Mitochondrial Transplantation
summary: Engineered delivery of donor mitochondria to selected cell types, exemplified by binder-based MitoCatch targeting of mitochondria to disease-affected cells.
mechanism_summary: Targeting binders attach donor mitochondria to selected recipient cell surfaces or bridge mitochondria to target cells, aiming to improve uptake, cytosolic exposure, intracellular mitochondrial dynamics, and rescue of mitochondrial-dysfunction-linked cellular injury.

open_questions: Whether cell-type targeting improves outcomes in aged tissues rather than acute or genetic injury models
How tissue distribution, immune response, and mitochondrial persistence behave in vivo
Whether targeted uptake translates to durable bioenergetic and functional benefit
Which cell types are realistic therapeutic targets for aging-relevant disease
